instructions: Speak with a clear, warm, feminine-presenting Korean teaching voice. Use natural standard Seoul Korean at a calm teaching pace. Say only the Korean input exactly once. Keep the final consonant audible through the correct mouth closure, but do not exaggerate it and never add a trailing vowel. Do not spell Hangul, translate, explain, or read punctuation aloud.

items.instructions: Say only the Korean word 산, meaning mountain, exactly once. End cleanly on final ㄴ with the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge. Do not add a vowel after ㄴ.
Say only the Korean word 몸, meaning body, exactly once. Close both lips fully on final ㅁ and stop there. Do not release into an extra vowel.
Say only the Korean word 공, meaning ball, exactly once. End on the back nasal final ㅇ, like ng in song, without an added vowel.
Say only the Korean word 물, meaning water, exactly once. Finish with the Korean final ㄹ, tongue tip touching the roof of the mouth, without an added vowel.
Say only the Korean word 반, meaning half, exactly once. Keep ㅂ + ㅏ clear and finish on final ㄴ without a trailing vowel.
Say only the Korean word 밤, meaning night, exactly once. Keep ㅂ + ㅏ clear and close both lips on final ㅁ without a trailing vowel.
Say only the Korean word 방, meaning room, exactly once. Keep ㅂ + ㅏ clear and finish with the back nasal final ㅇ without a trailing vowel.
Say only the Korean word 발, meaning foot, exactly once. Keep ㅂ + ㅏ clear and finish on the Korean final ㄹ without a trailing vowel.
Say only 감사합니다 exactly once as a natural, polite thank-you. Use standard Seoul pronunciation and apply the natural nasalization in 합니다, which sounds like 함니다.
